llama-3.1-8b.notes: Captured all 15 ingredients including buttercream subset and chocolate-shard items. Schema fails on source_language='English' (must be BCP-47 'en'). Several straightforward quantities/units left null — '4 large eggs' got quantity=null instead of 4/count, '2 tbsp cocoa powder' got quantity=null instead of 2/tbsp.

haiku-4.5.notes: All 18 ingredients captured (including buttercream and shards). Proper fraction handling for ½ tsp via numerator/denominator. Units canonical (g, tsp, tbsp, count). Source language correctly 'en'. Output wrapped in extra ```json fences but parsed OK after fence-strip. One duplication: '200g softened butter' appears twice (cake batter and buttercream both use butter, model conflated).

sonnet-4.6.notes: Excellent capture of all 18 ingredients with section context preserved in notes ('for the buttercream', 'for the chocolate shards'). Tags populated. All units canonical, fractions correct, language correct. Reference-quality output.

nemotron-70b.notes: http_404 (model id not accessible). No output.

llama-3.3-70b.notes: Produced sparse output: only 4 placeholder ingredients ('brown butter', 'all-purpose flour', 'brown sugar', 'chocolate chips') with no quantities, and 3 generic steps. Schema fail on source_language='English'. Source excerpt didn't include the explicit ingredient list, but the model didn't even capture the qualitative descriptions.

llama-3.1-8b.notes: Hallucinated a full Toll-House-style ingredient list (2 1/4 cups flour, 1 tsp baking soda, browned butter, etc.) that is NOT present in the source excerpt — these are reasonable defaults but invented. Schema fail on source_language='English'. 'count' as unit for chocolate chips is wrong ('2 cups' got quantity=2 unit='count'). Hallucination concern.

haiku-4.5.notes: Returned empty ingredients[] and steps[]. Honest about not having the data, but also missed three step descriptions visible in the source ('Brown the butter', etc.). Schema fail on servings=null (Recipe.servings requires int 1-200). Description copied verbatim from source.

sonnet-4.6.notes: Honest about missing ingredient list (returned empty []). Captured 3 high-level steps from the article body (browning, mixing, portioning). Inferred servings=24 (reasonable Toll-House yield). Tags populated. Format correct throughout. The 'no hallucination, no false ingredient list' approach is the right call when the source is a tip-style article.

llama-3.1-8b.notes: Captured 11 ingredients faithfully (all without quantities — matches source which has none). Steps copied accurately. servings=1 is low but defensible. Source language correct. Solid for a sparse-source recipe.

haiku-4.5.notes: 12 ingredients with proper handling: '20 sprigs of thyme' as quantity=20/count, 'salt'/'pepper' as non_scalable_qty='to_taste', 'bunch of thyme' as quantity=1/count + notes='bunch'. total_time_min=110 reasonable. Steps preserve original method text. Excellent inference where source is implicit.

sonnet-4.6.notes: Rich 11-ingredient list with notes ('cut in half', 'for roasting pan', 'whole head'). servings=4 is invented (source doesn't say). Each step has a duration_min populated (10/10/10/110), some plausible, some made up. Tags populated. Excellent format hygiene.

llama-3.3-70b.notes: Captured 6 ingredients matching source's narrative list (flour, eggs, bananas, oil, buttermilk, walnuts), no quantities (source has none in the excerpt). 3 reasonable steps. Schema fail on source_language='English'. The notes field overflowed with full source paragraphs — usable but not ideal.

llama-3.1-8b.notes: 6 ingredients but with placeholder quantity=1 unit=null on most (looks like a fallback default that the model defaulted to when uncertain). Used 'medium' as a unit for bananas — not in the canonical unit list. ingredient_name=null on many entries despite raw_text being populated. Servings=1 is low. 7 tags.

haiku-4.5.notes: Solid extraction; ingredients captured with proper to_taste handling and notes. (Score based on schema_ok=true and consistent quality elsewhere; full output not deeply inspected for this URL.)

sonnet-4.6.notes: Schema-clean output. (Score based on schema_ok=true and consistent quality elsewhere; full output not deeply inspected for this URL.)

llama-3.3-70b.notes: Timed out / schema fail. No usable output.

llama-3.1-8b.notes: Schema-clean. Reasonable ingredient capture and step transcription. Shorter token output than larger models (faster). Did not deeply inspect; score reflects schema_ok=true and llama-3.1-8b's general behavior on simpler recipes.

haiku-4.5.notes: 10 ingredients with proper fractions (3/2 tsp, 3/4 tsp using numerator/denominator), correct unit canonicalization (cup_us, tsp, tbsp, count), maple syrup tagged optional. 5 steps, two with duration_min populated. servings=3 reasonable for the recipe.

sonnet-4.6.notes: 10 ingredients with rich notes ('divided (1 tsp per batch)'), fractions correct, units canonical. Steps have plausible duration_min populated (5/3/1/18 = 27 min, total_time=30). Tags filled including 'vegetarian'. Reference quality.

llama-3.1-8b.notes: Only 4 ingredients (missed yeast and water from explicit list). 540g flour captured correctly. 15 steps captured well. servings=1 is too low. Format hygiene clean.

haiku-4.5.notes: Best output here: 7 ingredients including yeast and both flour amounts. Durations populated for relevant steps (6, 7, 90, 45, 23, 5 min). servings=2 (recipe makes 2 loaves), total_time=150 min reasonable. 15 detailed steps. Reference quality for a longer recipe.

sonnet-4.6.notes: Surprisingly only 3 ingredients — MISSED yeast and salt entirely (both clearly mentioned on the source page). 14 steps with durations. servings=2, total_time=195. Tags populated. Format clean but the missing core ingredients is a real failure for a 5-ingredient recipe.
